ranking.description: The current ranking of the team.

Match.title: Match
Match.description: Represents a Faustball match between two teams.

team1Score.description: The score of team 1 in the match.

team2Score.description: The score of team 2 in the match.

matchDateTime.description: The date and time the match was played.

teamId.description: Reference to the Team the Player plays for. (Relationship: Team 1:N Player)

position.description: The playing position of the player.

id.description: Unique identifier for the cash register.

teamId.description: Reference to the team. (Relationship: Team 1:1 Mannschaftskasse)

balance.description: The current balance of the team's cash register.

definition.description: Collection of Faustball teams. Each document represents a team.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier of the team.

definition.description: Subcollection of players for a specific team. Each document represents a player in that team.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier of the team.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the player.

definition.description: Subcollection of matches for a specific team. Each document represents a match the team played in.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier of the team.

params.name: matchId
params.description: The unique identifier of the match.

definition.description: Subcollection representing the team's cash register. Each document represents the cash register for the corresponding team.

params.name: teamId
params.description: The unique identifier of the team.

params.name: mannschaftskasseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the cash register (should typically be the same as the teamId to ensure a 1:1 relationship).

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to manage Faustball teams, matches, players, and team cash registers. The structure prioritizes Authorization Independence by avoiding `get()` calls in security rules. This is achieved by using path-based ownership for user-related data, enabling atomic operations and simplifying security rule management.

**Authorization Independence:**
Path-based ownership (`/teams/{teamId}/...`) establishes clear ownership, making security rules simple and efficient without needing to retrieve parent document data.

**Structural Segregation:**
The structure uses dedicated collections for each entity (teams, matches, players, cash registers), ensuring that all documents within a collection share the same security requirements.

**Access Modeling:**
-   **Team Data:** Uses a top-level `/teams` collection. Each team has a subcollection for players and a subcollection for matches.
-   **Player Data:** Players are stored within the `/teams/{teamId}/players` collection, clearly associating them with their respective teams.
-   **Match Data:** Matches are stored within the `/teams/{teamId}/matches` collection, associating them with a team.
-   **Mannschaftskasse (Cash Register):**  Each team has a dedicated document in the `/teams/{teamId}/mannschaftskasse` collection, ensuring that the cash register data is directly associated with the team. This is a 1:1 relationship.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
The structure supports secure `list` operations by ensuring that security rules can be applied directly to collection reads, without needing to filter data based on document content. Path-based ownership enables secure listing of resources associated with a specific team.

**Invariants:**
The structure supports data integrity by enforcing clear ownership and relationships between entities. For example, a `Player` document's `teamId` field always points to an existing `Team` document. Similarly, each `Mannschaftskasse` document is tied to a specific `Team` via the `teamId`.
